visual_checklist_notes.reference_match: The generated drawing does not visually match the same-name reference: view outlines, dimensional scheme, title block, and note placement differ substantially.
visual_checklist_notes.view_layout: Four views are visible, but the generated views are bunched and scaled/positioned unlike the reference; the main long views no longer read as the reference front/top layout.
visual_checklist_notes.display_dimensions: The screenshot shows many dimensions, but the visible dimension scheme is not reference-like and the latest trace still loses semantic coverage after final arrange/reopen.
visual_checklist_notes.dimension_readability: Dimensions, leaders, arrows, and text are clustered around the central views; several labels overlap visually or are too dense for manufacturing review.
visual_checklist_notes.title_block: The generated lower-right title/data area is not equivalent to the compact reference title block and remains structurally incomplete.
visual_checklist_notes.manufacturing_notes: Notes are present, but they do not match the reference note style/placement and are not visually sufficient as manufacturing instructions.

findings: Application Drawing Review UI screenshot was inspected side by side with the reference image.
The new run improved live DrawingView recovery and produced current-run SLDDRW/PDF/DXF/PNG artifacts, but visual acceptance still fails.
Layout scale solving moved in the wrong direction and exhausted the scale ladder; the final generated views are much smaller/bunched compared with the reference.
DisplayDim count reached 21, but the visual result remains unreadable/crowded and semantic target coverage still regressed after the final arrange pass.
API/reference metrics are supporting evidence only; the UI screenshot review is the final gate and remains FAIL.
entries.required_correction: Fix the 006 reference-outline scale solver so it chooses larger, reference-matching outlines instead of shrinking through 1:10/1:20/1:50; preserve hole_diameter and hole_pitch through final arrange/reopen; then regenerate title block and manufacturing notes in the compact reference style before another locked 006-only CAD rerun.
